title: Dean's Forum: Into the Weeds Again: Creeds and Baptism
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Sermon: "Dean's Forum: Into the Weeds Again: Creeds and Baptism"
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
October 27, 2019 | Preacher: Broderick Greer

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon was preached on the Last Sunday after Pentecost (Proper 25), falling within Ordinary Time in the Christian calendar. The Revised Common Lectionary readings for this Sunday were typically Jeremiah 31:7-9, Psalm 126, Hebrews 7:23-28, and Mark 10:46-52, focusing on themes of healing and divine restoration. The liturgical year was moving toward Advent (which would begin December 1, 2019), with only five Sundays remaining in Ordinary Time.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in the immediate aftermath of General Convention 2018 (held in July 2018, Austin, Texas), with ongoing diocesan implementation of those resolutions. The Diocese of Colorado was under the leadership of Bishop Robert J. Wright Jr., who had served since 2012. This was a period of relative stability in the domestic church, though broader Anglican Communion conversations about biblical interpretation and sexuality remained theologically contentious—directly relevant to a sermon about creeds and baptismal theology.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing typical autumn weather patterns in late October, with daytime temperatures around 50-60°F. The city was focused on local ballot initiatives ahead of the November 2019 election, including tax measures and school funding questions. Saint John's Cathedral, located in the Capitol Hill neighborhood, was situated in Denver's historic downtown core, an area undergoing significant gentrification and renovation during this period.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Trump administration was in its third year, with the 2020 presidential election campaign actively underway. The week before October 27 saw continued impeachment inquiry coverage in the House as the Ukraine controversy deepened. The Denver Broncos had just concluded their Week 8 NFL game (losing to the Kansas City Chiefs 23-3 on October 28), disappointing fans in a struggling season. Additionally, the World Series was in progress, with the Washington Nationals ultimately defeating the Houston Astros in Game 7 on October 30.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The Syrian conflict remained active with Turkish military operations ongoing in northern Syria following the U.S. withdrawal announcement, creating a humanitarian crisis with displaced populations. The Hong Kong pro-democracy protests were intensifying, with escalating clashes between demonstrators and police throughout October 2019. Brexit negotiations continued as Britain approached the October 31 deadline, with Prime Minister Boris Johnson's withdrawal agreement awaiting parliamentary votes and approval.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America in late October 2019 was in a season of political polarization and anxiety, with the impeachment process dominating media coverage and social discourse. The mood was transitional—Halloween approaching, holiday shopping season beginning, but with underlying tension regarding the 2020 election and deep partisan divisions. In Denver specifically, the city was growing rapidly and wrestling with questions of identity, affordability, and community change—issues that would resonate with liturgical questions about tradition, creedal inheritance, and baptismal belonging that Greer's sermon addresses.
